GenAiPromptTemplate.description: This prompt template adds up the total number of sources considered in the report generated.

templateVersions.content:
Your job is to count the number of sources from the citations from the JSON objects below. Please only output the number of sources. For example if you identify 7 sources your output should just be: 7.
Here is contextual information about the JSON objects you will count sources from:
JSON Response Object Description
The JSON object represents a response generated by an AI model. It contains metadata about the response, token usage statistics, cited sources, and the generated content. Here's a detailed breakdown of each field in the JSON object:
Top-Level Keys id Type: String Description: A globally unique identifier for this specific response. It serves as a reference for tracking or auditing the response. Example: "b2378da9-bf38-4a92-a341-4c2558363e95" model Type: String Description: The name or identifier of the AI model that generated the response. This value specifies the particular version or type of the model used. Example: "sonar-pro" created Type: Integer (Unix Timestamp) Description: The time the response was generated, represented as a Unix timestamp (seconds since January 1, 1970). Example: 1748462839 (This value corresponds to a specific date and time.) usage Type: Object Description: Contains metrics detailing token usage and the search context size for this specific request-response interaction. Fields: prompt_tokens: Type: Integer Description: Number of tokens used in the input prompt provided to the AI. Example: 150 completion_tokens: Type: Integer Description: Number of tokens used in the AI-generated response (completion). Example: 406 total_tokens: Type: Integer Description: Total number of tokens used in the interaction (sum of prompt_tokens and completion_tokens). Example: 556 search_context_size: Type: String Description: Indicates the size or scope of the search context utilized by the model to generate the response. Example: "high" citations Type: Array of Strings Description: A list of URLs representing sources or materials cited in the generated response. These provide attribution or additional reference for the information included in the completion. Example:
[ "https://www.salesforce.com/news/", "https://www.salesforceben.com/category/news/", "https://www.salesforce.com/blog/what-does-salesforce-do/", "https://investor.salesforce.com/overview/default.aspx", "https://www.the-independent.com/topic/salesforce" ]
6. object Type: String Description: Specifies the type of object being returned by the API. In this case, "chat.completion" indicates that the object contains a conversational completion generated by the AI. Example: "chat.completion" choices Type: Array of Objects Description: Contains one or more generated completions (responses) from the AI model. Each element in the array is a possible "choice" of response. Fields (per object in the array): index: Type: Integer Description: The index or position of the choice in the array. This allows multiple responses to be differentiated if more than one choice is returned. Example: 0 finish_reason: Type: String Description: Indicates why the response generation process ended. For example, "stop" means the response completed naturally. Example: "stop" message: Type: Object Description: Contains the main content of the generated response and metadata about the speaker. Fields: role: Type: String Description: The role of the speaker in the conversation. In this case, "assistant" indicates that the response was generated by the AI assistant. Example: "assistant" content: Type: String Description: The text content of the generated response. This is the main body of the message and provides the AI's answer or explanation. Example: "Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM (Customer Relationship Management) platform that helps businesses use artificial intelligence and data to improve their customer connections..." delta: Type: Object Description: Used for streaming responses to provide incremental updates to the generated content. In this example, it is empty, indicating no incremental updates were provided. Fields: role: Type: String Description: The role of the speaker for the incremental update (if applicable). Example: "assistant" content: Type: String Description: The incremental content update (empty in this example). Example: ""
Summary Table
Structured Data : The JSON object is highly structured, making it easy to parse and process. Citations: The inclusion of cited sources ensures transparency and credibility. Token Usage: Detailed token usage statistics are provided, which are useful for billing or optimization. Message Content: The core of the response is in the choices array, specifically in the message.content field.
Here are the JSON objects to count the number of sources from:
{!$Input:Company_Overview}
{!$Input:Positioning_and_Landscape}
{!$Input:Strategic_Priorities}
{!$Input:Tech_Insights}

Your job is to count the total number of UNIQUE sources cited across the JSON inputs provided below. The inputs are JSON responses from an Agentforce Employee Agent. You must parse the JSON to find the citations. INSTRUCTIONS: Look for the key "citedReferences" inside the "value" object of each JSON input. Inside that list, look for the field "value" (this contains the URL). Extract all URLs from all inputs. Deduplicate the list (count each unique URL only once). Output ONLY the final integer count. For example: 7. SCHEMA CONTEXT: The JSON follows this structure: type: "InformMessage" value: message: (The text summary you should ignore for counting) citedReferences: (A list of objects) z0type: "link" value: "https://example.com/source-url" (THIS IS THE SOURCE TO COUNT) label: "The visible label of the link" INPUTS TO ANALYZE: Input 1 (Company Overview): {!$Input:Company_Overview} Input 2 (Positioning): {!$Input:Positioning_and_Landscape} Input 3 (Priorities): {!$Input:Strategic_Priorities} Input 4 (Tech Insights): {!$Input:Tech_Insights}

Your job is to count the number of sources from the citations from the JSON objects below. Please only output the number of sources. For example if you identify 7 sources your output should just be: 7.
Here is contextual information about the JSON objects you will count sources from:
JSON Response Object Description
The JSON object represents a response generated by an AI model. It contains metadata about the response, token usage statistics, cited sources, and the generated content. Here's a detailed breakdown of each field in the JSON object:
Top-Level Keys id Type: String Description: A globally unique identifier for this specific response. It serves as a reference for tracking or auditing the response. Example: "b2378da9-bf38-4a92-a341-4c2558363e95" model Type: String Description: The name or identifier of the AI model that generated the response. This value specifies the particular version or type of the model used. Example: "sonar-pro" created Type: Integer (Unix Timestamp) Description: The time the response was generated, represented as a Unix timestamp (seconds since January 1, 1970). Example: 1748462839 (This value corresponds to a specific date and time.) usage Type: Object Description: Contains metrics detailing token usage and the search context size for this specific request-response interaction. Fields: prompt_tokens: Type: Integer Description: Number of tokens used in the input prompt provided to the AI. Example: 150 completion_tokens: Type: Integer Description: Number of tokens used in the AI-generated response (completion). Example: 406 total_tokens: Type: Integer Description: Total number of tokens used in the interaction (sum of prompt_tokens and completion_tokens). Example: 556 search_context_size: Type: String Description: Indicates the size or scope of the search context utilized by the model to generate the response. Example: "high" citations Type: Array of Strings Description: A list of URLs representing sources or materials cited in the generated response. These provide attribution or additional reference for the information included in the completion. Example:
[ "https://www.salesforce.com/news/", "https://www.salesforceben.com/category/news/", "https://www.salesforce.com/blog/what-does-salesforce-do/", "https://investor.salesforce.com/overview/default.aspx", "https://www.the-independent.com/topic/salesforce" ]
6. object Type: String Description: Specifies the type of object being returned by the API. In this case, "chat.completion" indicates that the object contains a conversational completion generated by the AI. Example: "chat.completion" choices Type: Array of Objects Description: Contains one or more generated completions (responses) from the AI model. Each element in the array is a possible "choice" of response. Fields (per object in the array): index: Type: Integer Description: The index or position of the choice in the array. This allows multiple responses to be differentiated if more than one choice is returned. Example: 0 finish_reason: Type: String Description: Indicates why the response generation process ended. For example, "stop" means the response completed naturally. Example: "stop" message: Type: Object Description: Contains the main content of the generated response and metadata about the speaker. Fields: role: Type: String Description: The role of the speaker in the conversation. In this case, "assistant" indicates that the response was generated by the AI assistant. Example: "assistant" content: Type: String Description: The text content of the generated response. This is the main body of the message and provides the AI's answer or explanation. Example: "Salesforce is the #1 AI CRM (Customer Relationship Management) platform that helps businesses use artificial intelligence and data to improve their customer connections..." delta: Type: Object Description: Used for streaming responses to provide incremental updates to the generated content. In this example, it is empty, indicating no incremental updates were provided. Fields: role: Type: String Description: The role of the speaker for the incremental update (if applicable). Example: "assistant" content: Type: String Description: The incremental content update (empty in this example). Example: ""
Summary Table
Structured Data : The JSON object is highly structured, making it easy to parse and process. Citations: The inclusion of cited sources ensures transparency and credibility. Token Usage: Detailed token usage statistics are provided, which are useful for billing or optimization. Message Content: The core of the response is in the choices array, specifically in the message.content field.
Here are the JSON objects to count the number of sources from:
{!$Input:Company_Overview}
{!$Input:Positioning_and_Landscape}
{!$Input:Strategic_Priorities}
{!$Input:Tech_Insights}
